summary: Medical hydrology involves medically supervised aquatic therapies aimed at treating specific pathologies such as joint pain and inflammation through defined physiological mechanisms; spa therapy focuses on aesthetic and recreational benefits without diagnostic or curative intent or integration into standard healthcare practices. These differences establish them as distinctly different modalities—one rooted in medicine, the other in wellness tourism.
